name: Carrot Cake (Aargau)
recipeInstructions:
(Units: 100 g = 3 1/2 oz; 1 dl = 3 1/2 fl oz = 2/5 cup; 180°F = 350°F;
200°F = 400°F; 230°F = 450°F; 250°F = 475°F; 2.5 cm = 1 inch)
The Story:
The cultivation of vegetables in Aargau is one of the main reasons for the
existence of a thriving canning/bottling industry. In this area, the carrot
is the Queen of all vegetables and it has attributes which allow it to be
used everywhere, even in desserts.
This carrot cake is now a Swiss classic, its reputation having been
extended long ago far beyond the borders of the canton. Some maintain that
the cake is better two days after baking as the carrots keep it moist.
The Recipe:
1. Beat together the egg yolks, sugar, lemon juice and lemon zest until light.
2. Add the finely grated carrots and almonds.
3. Mix in the cornflour, cinnamon, ground cloves and baking powder. Then add the kirsch.
4. Mix or beat until smooth.
5. Beat the egg whites until stiff and carefully fold them in.
6. Butter a tin (24 cm diameter, 5 cm high) and dust with flour.
7. Turn the mixture into the prepared tin.
8. Bake in the oven for 50...60 minutes at 180°F.
9. When cooked, brush the cake with hot apricot glaze and thin fondant icing.
10. Decorate with small marzipan carrots.
